title: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (HST) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. (HST) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 64/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% growth rate is estimated based on the recent free cash flow generation and the fundamental outlook for the REIT - Hotel & Motel sector.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A discount rate of 9.0% reflects the company's cost of capital, weighing systemic risks and consistent cash generation.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 2.0% is selected as the terminal growth rate, aligning with long-term macroeconomic GDP expectations.

thesisParagraphs: Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. currently generates stable free cash flows, which are critical for its valuation in the REIT - Hotel & Motel space. At a current price of $19.1, the market has embedded specific growth expectations into the stock.
Using a discounted cash flow model, we estimate an intrinsic value of $26.99. The current valuation suggests a margin of safety of 41.29%, leading to a UNDERVALUED verdict.
verdictFooter: Based on actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why was a 3.0% growth rate used?
faq.answer: The rate aligns with normalized historical and projected fundamental growth trends in the REIT - Hotel & Motel sector.

faq.question: What does the discount rate reflect?
faq.answer: The WACC represents the blended cost of equity and debt, capturing the risk profile of the cash flows.

faq.question: Is this financial advice?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is an AI-generated model based on quantitative inputs and rigid formulas.

prospectVerdictDetail: Host Hotels & Resorts benefits from an exceptional portfolio of luxury and upper-upscale properties. However, its high sensitivity to macroeconomic cycles and corporate travel budgets results in a moderate economic prospect.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Competitive momentum is steady, buoyed by the sustained resilience of luxury leisure travel and a gradual recovery in large-scale group conventions.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Host's top-line performance tracks closely with upper-upscale lodging trends. Growth is highly dependent on RevPAR expansion rather than aggressively adding new properties.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The company maintains a strong footprint in major urban centers and resort destinations. Its scale as the largest lodging REIT provides a distinct advantage in asset negotiations.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: In the luxury and resort segments, Host exhibits solid pricing power, able to drive average daily rates (ADR) higher during peak seasons and economic expansions.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Velocity is defined by cyclical renovations and strategic re-brandings rather than traditional product launches, keeping its portfolio fresh and competitive.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Host's moat is primarily built on the irreplicable nature of its prime real estate assets, though the business model remains highly cyclical and capital intensive.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Switching costs for individual guests are low, but for massive group conventions, the logistical complexity of moving to a different venue provides significant stickiness.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Host benefits indirectly from the massive loyalty programs (like Marriott Bonvoy) of the operators that manage its properties, driving consistent occupancy.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Regulatory advantages are limited to local zoning laws that restrict new competitive hotel construction in prime urban or coastal areas.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: The business is exceptionally capital intensive. Maintaining luxury standards requires constant, heavy reinvestment (FF&E), which eats into free cash flow.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment is highly tethered to macroeconomic data. While the portfolio is premium, investors remain wary of the inherent cyclicality of the lodging sector.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Estimates (specifically Adjusted Funds From Operations, or AFFO) are heavily scrutinized for signs of consumer exhaustion, leading to frequent but minor revisions based on macro data.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative oscillates between praise for its premium resort assets and anxiety over its exposure to lagging urban corporate travel hubs.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has maintained a fortress balance sheet relative to peers, allowing them to reinstate and grow the dividend while opportunistically recycling capital.

prospectKeyRisks: A broader macroeconomic recession significantly reducing corporate and leisure travel.
Sustained elevated interest rates impacting debt refinancing costs and capitalization rates.
High fixed costs associated with maintaining luxury properties compressing margins during downturns.
prospectKeyCatalysts: A resurgence in group bookings and international inbound travel boosting Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR).
Strategic dispositions of lower-tier assets to fund stock buybacks or special dividends.
Favorable supply dynamics as new hotel construction remains constrained by high financing costs.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored HST at 63/100 and Opus at 63/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
